calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-16-2009 Off Calendar

Petition To Enforce Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary William Clark Jr
Off Calendar Aug-05-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jerome Marks for Marks & McAndrew. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Trustee).

The Court granted the Petition To Enforce Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary, (signing the order for the same in open court). The Court also granted the Petition For Approval Of Settlement, (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1377-1379 9th Ave., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar May-11-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch, Successor Trustee.

The sale was confirmed at $1,050,000.00; an additional bond was set at $987,000.00.
Order signed in court.

Comm. Sue Kaplan, CSR: LaVena Ward #7077, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Gomez.

Petition For Order Directing Trustee To Exercise Powers In Accordance With Order Filed September 19, 2008 Notwithstanding Appeal
Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Advanced From Jan-07-09 To Nov-05-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Trustee To Exercise Powers In Accordance With Order Filed September 19, 2008 Notwithstanding Appeal
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Granted Per Written Order

Petition Of William Clark For Formal Discovery And Evidentiary Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Le Sagatelyan for Jeffrey Paquette. Henry Glasser for William Clark. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

The Court considered continuing the evidentiary hearing and the Petition For Order Directing Trustee To Exersize Powers for one month pending the appeal. Mr. Der stated that the appeal would take some time, that there was interest in the property and requested going forward with the sale of the property. Mr. Glasser agreed that the entire matter should be stayed and stated that the petitioner had the burden of proving that a stay would cause injury or loss. Mr. Sagatelyan stated that his Client had a 50% interst in the property, that the matter had gone on for a long time and that it had caused economic harm to his Client. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Weatherly.

Ex Parte Petition For Order Shortening Time For Petition For Order Directing Trustee To Exercise Powers In Accordance With Order Dated September 19, 2008 Notwithstanding Appeal
Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch. Jared Dreyfus for William Clark also present in court.

Mr. Der stated that he needed the order shortening time because the sale was progressing, that he need to know if they could proceed or if Mr. Clark needed an undertaking, and stated that three disclosure packets had been sent out in regards to the property. Mr. Dreyfus stated that he objected to the order shortening time, that he had not seen the petition, and that the harm may fall on his client in the way of capital gains taxes. Additional arguments were made and upon there conclusion, the Court granted the Ex Parte Application for Order Shortening Time (signing an order for the same in open court). The hearing is advanced from January 7, 2009 to November 5, 2008 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court instructed Mr. Der to cooperate wtih Mr. Dreyfus. Mr. Dreyfus' papers are due November 3, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ J. Alameda.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-11-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Clark in propria persona.

No other appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-20-2008 Continued To Sep-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Clark in Pro Per. Levon Sagatelyn for Jeffrey Paquette (Beneficiary). Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Petitioner).

Mr. Clark stated that he was not opposed to paying the rent, but that he believed the rent was paid by not receiving the income distribution of $750.00 per month. Mr. Der stated that there was no agreement as to the offsetting of rents. The Court suggested the Parties try to work it out and stated that no ruling would be made at this time. The matter was continued to September 10, 2008 and will be taken under submission on that date. Mr. Der will file a status report.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bonner.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-06-2008 Continued To Aug-20-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Clark in Pro Per. Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch. Levon Sagatelyan for Jeffrey Paquette (Beneficiary).

Mr. Der reported that mediation was not successful, that mediation had not been rescheduled and stated that the matter should go forward and submitted on the pleadings. Mr. Clark verified his report in open court. In addition, Mr. Clark stated that his response/objections were not addressed in mediation. The matter was continued to August 20, 2008. The Court directed Mr. Clark to pay the back rent or satisfy the Attorney's as to why it should not be paid.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Leong.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-08-2008 Continued To Mar-04-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch. Jerome Marks for William Clark. Jeffrey Paquette also present.

The Court stated that it received the objection at 4:00 pm yesterday. Mr. Der indicated that he did not have a chance to review the objection. Mr. Marks suggested continuing the matter on the objections for 45 days, to direct the parties to mediation, and stated that the parties would attempt to resolve the matter. Mr. Der stated that Mr. Paquette wanted to be represented by Counsel, but could not afford one. Mr. Mark also stated that Mr. Clark and Mr. Spencer objected to the sale of the property. The Court referred the matter to Pro Bono Mediation, (signing the order for the same in open court). Mediation must be completed no later than February 26, 2008. The matter was continued to March 4, 2008 for status on mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Attorneys' Fees And Costs Incurred In The Initition Of Court Supervision Of Trust.

Petition For Appt Of Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-29-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Doug Penson appearing for and with William Clark. Ernest Der appearing for Debra Dolch, temporary successor trustee. John Hall appearing for and with Mr. Paquette. Mr. Clark wants to be appointed as successor trustee. Mr. Paquette does not want Mr. Clark to serve but he wants Debra Dolch appointed. Ms. Dolch accepts the appointment. Court is appointing Debra Dolch as the successor trustee. Bond is fixed in the amount of $62,000.00. Order signed. Other petitions are off calendar. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR#1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
